name: Cardiology
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat heart and blood vessel disorders such as heart attacks, arrhythmias, and hypertension.

name: Neurology
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat disorders of the nervous system including brain, spinal cord, and nerves.

name: Pediatrics
responsibilities: Provide medical care for infants, children, and adolescents.

name: Psychiatry
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat mental health disorders through therapy and medication.

name: Orthopedics
responsibilities: Treat musculoskeletal system issues including bones, joints, ligaments, and muscles.

name: Ophthalmology
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat eye diseases and perform eye surgeries.

name: Otolaryngology
responsibilities: Treat conditions related to the ear, nose, throat, and related structures.

name: Gastroenterology
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat digestive system disorders including stomach, intestines, and liver.

name: Pulmonology
responsibilities: Manage respiratory system conditions such as asthma, bronchitis, and lung infections.

name: Nephrology
responsibilities: Treat kidney-related diseases and manage dialysis treatments.

name: Hematology
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat blood disorders such as anemia, clotting disorders, and leukemia.

name: Rheumatology
responsibilities: Treat autoimmune and inflammatory diseases affecting joints and connective tissue.

name: Anesthesiology
responsibilities: Administer anesthesia and monitor patients during surgical procedures.

name: Radiology
responsibilities: Use imaging techniques like X-rays, MRI, and CT scans to diagnose diseases.

name: Emergency Medicine
responsibilities: Provide immediate care for acute illnesses and injuries in emergency settings.
